https://media.karpowitsch.org/troysd-boa.paragraph: Troy School District's FY2025–26 General Fund Budget Amendment #1, presented January 13, 2026 by CFO Dan Trudel, CPA. It raises budgeted revenues to $197.9M and expenditures to $203.8M — a planned $6.0M deficit that draws the ending fund balance down to $27.7M (13.59%). The revisions are driven largely by state retirement (MPSERS) funding shifts, including the last-minute elimination of the $1.0M 147a(1) categorical and the loss of $5.1M in one-time 147a(4) pension-offset money.
https://media.karpowitsch.org/troysd-boa.page:
Purpose
First budget amendment of FY2025–26, updating the General Fund and the district's other funds to reflect final state-aid, federal grant, and ISD allocations plus anticipated year-end expenditures.

Enrollment & foundation
Enrollment is 12,069.81 FTE, down 4.19 from the original budget. Foundation revenue rises $558,308 to $130,788,472.

MPSERS / state retirement
FY2025 included $5,055,278 of one-time 147a(4) pension-offset money that is eliminated in FY2026. For FY2026 the state removed the $1,007,246 147a(1) categorical at the last minute; employees no longer contribute the 3% healthcare premium (now absorbed by the district); pension UAAL costs fell 5.75% while the healthcare-subsidy expense rose 2.58% and normal cost rose up to 1.72%.

General Fund (Original → Amendment #1)
Revenues $190.5M → $197.9M; expenditures $194.3M → $203.8M; change in fund balance –$3.7M → –$6.0M; ending fund balance $29.2M → $27.7M (15.05% → 13.59%).

Other funds (amended change in fund balance)
Community Service –$0.55M; Preschool –$1.28M; Troy Career Center break-even; Food Service –$1.86M; Debt Fund +$0.20M. Prepared by Dan Trudel (dtrudel@troy.k12.mi.us).
https://media.karpowitsch.org/troysd-boa.verbose:
Overview
This workshop presentation is the district's first General Fund Budget Amendment for fiscal year 2025–26, delivered by CFO Dan Trudel, CPA. Amendments true up the adopted budget to final revenue allocations and anticipated year-end spending. Four drivers are cited: categorical funding adjusted to the state aid status report; federal grant funding adjusted to final allocations; ISD-related funding adjusted to final allocations; and purchased services, salaries, and benefits adjusted to anticipated final expenditures.

Enrollment and foundation revenue
Blended enrollment is 12,069.81 FTE, a decrease of 4.19 from the original 12,074.00. The foundation allowance revenue increases by $558,308, from $130,230,164 to $130,788,472.

MPSERS and state retirement changes
The presentation devotes significant attention to Michigan Public School Employees' Retirement System (MPSERS) dynamics. In FY2025 the state provided $5,055,278 in 147a(4) funds to offset retirement expenses — a one-time categorical payment that does not roll into the future foundation allowance and is eliminated for FY2026. For FY2026, the state eliminated the 147a(1) categorical at the last minute, a $1,007,246 reduction; separately, employees no longer contribute the 3% healthcare premium, which the employer has absorbed this year, and anticipated state help to cover that 3% did not materialize. On the cost side, pension UAAL expenditures for districts were reduced 5.75%, the healthcare-premium subsidy expense increased 2.58%, and pension normal cost increased up to 1.72%.

General Fund bottom line
Budgeted revenues rise from $190,545,984 to $197,935,255, while expenditures rise from $194,285,613 to $203,763,818. The planned change in fund balance widens from –$3,739,629 to –$6,003,563. Beginning fund balance is $33,713,795 and the ending fund balance falls to $27,710,232, or 13.59% of expenditures (down from a projected 15.05%).

Other funds
The amendment also revises the Community Service Fund (ending balance ~$1.48M, change –$0.55M), the Preschool Fund (change –$1.28M), the Troy Career Center Fund (break-even), the Food Service Fund (ending balance ~$0.82M, change –$1.86M), and the Debt Fund (change +$0.20M, ending balance ~$4.17M). Contact: Dan Trudel, dtrudel@troy.k12.mi.us, Troy School District, 4400 Livernois Rd., Troy, MI 48098.
https://media.karpowitsch.org/troysd-boa.title: 4.a. Budget Presentation 1-13-26

https://media.karpowitsch.org/troysd-boa.paragraph: A March 17, 2026 resolution asking the Board to award the complete renovation of Boulan Park Middle School — architectural, mechanical, electrical, and structural work — for a total of $20,185,066 from the Capital Projects Fund. The award covers $18,350,066 in category contracts plus a 10% ($1,835,000) contingency, following a competitive RFP reviewed by Operations Director Rob Carson and the Barton Malow Company.
https://media.karpowitsch.org/troysd-boa.page:
Action requested
Under Business item 8.C, the Board is asked to accept a resolution awarding the complete renovation of Boulan Park Middle School and to approve the expenditure from the Capital Projects Fund.

Process
An RFP was issued for competitive bids covering architectural, mechanical, electrical, and structural work. Responses were reviewed by Rob Carson, Director of Operations, together with the Barton Malow Company, which recommended the awards below.

Award
Category awards total $18,350,066, with a 10% contingency of $1,835,000, for a total recommended award of $20,185,066.

Contractors by category
Demolition — North American Dismantling Corp. ($1,306,350); Concrete — Metropolitan Concrete Corp. ($1,265,555); Masonry — Brazen & Greer Masonry ($3,259,700); Structural Steel — Howard Structural Steel ($1,158,608); Carpentry — Hicks Construction Company ($1,688,008); Lockers — Rayhaven Group ($272,700); Food Service Equipment — Great Lakes Hotel Supply ($240,453); Fire Suppression — Jackson Associates ($528,500); Plumbing — Contrast Mechanical ($1,507,000); Mechanical — Contrast Mechanical ($3,768,000); Electrical — Gemini Electric ($2,973,685); Automatic Temperature Controls — Metro Controls ($381,507).
https://media.karpowitsch.org/troysd-boa.verbose:
Purpose
This is the suggested resolution for Business item 8.C at the March 17, 2026 regular meeting: consideration of the Boulan Park Renovation Project. It asks the Board to award a complete renovation of Boulan Park Middle School — encompassing architectural, mechanical, electrical, and structural work — and to approve the total as an expenditure of the Capital Projects Fund.

Procurement process
An RFP was issued to obtain competitive bids for the full renovation. Bid responses were reviewed by Rob Carson, the district's Director of Operations, in conjunction with the Barton Malow Company (construction manager), which produced the award recommendation. The resolution is drafted in standard form with mover/supporter and yes/no lines to be completed on the floor.

Recommended awards by trade category
• Demolition — North American Dismantling Corp. — $1,306,350.00
• Concrete — Metropolitan Concrete Corp. — $1,265,555.00
• Masonry — Brazen & Greer Masonry — $3,259,700.00
• Structural Steel — Howard Structural Steel — $1,158,608.00
• Carpentry — Hicks Construction Company — $1,688,008.00
• Lockers — Rayhaven Group — $272,700.00
• Food Service Equipment — Great Lakes Hotel Supply Company — $240,453.00
• Fire Suppression — Jackson Associates — $528,500.00
• Plumbing — Contrast Mechanical, LLC — $1,507,000.00
• Mechanical — Contrast Mechanical, LLC — $3,768,000.00
• Electrical — Gemini Electric Company, Inc. — $2,973,685.00
• Automatic Temperature Controls — Metro Controls Inc. — $381,507.00

Totals
Total category awards are $18,350,066.00. A 10% contingency of $1,835,000.00 brings the total recommended award to $20,185,066.00, to be approved as a Capital Projects Fund expenditure. Two mechanical scopes (plumbing and mechanical) are both awarded to Contrast Mechanical, and the largest single trade award is the $3.77M mechanical package.

https://media.karpowitsch.org/troysd-boa.paragraph: A March 2026 policy-update memo from MJ School Policy Services (Miller Johnson) recommending substantive revisions to the district's policy manual and administrative regulations. The headline change — Update 22.01 to Policy 2003, Education Records — implements new MCL 388.1764k / §164k student-survey requirements from the FY2025–26 State School Aid Act: parental inspection rights, opt-in consent for sensitive surveys, and public posting of survey questions and results, subject to FERPA.
https://media.karpowitsch.org/troysd-boa.page:
Source & format
A memo (dated March 9, 2026) from MJ School Policy Services to policy clients, delivering substantive updates to the policy manual and administrative regulations. Revisions are organized by manual section with individually numbered updates (e.g., Update 22.01); additions are shown in red, deletions in blue, and yellow highlights flag items needing district-specific input.

Headline update — 22.01, Policy 2003 (Education Records)
The FY2025–26 State School Aid Act adds MCL 388.1764k, imposing new student-survey requirements on districts and ISDs. Recommended revisions ensure compliance with §164k:
• Parents may inspect all materials used for any student survey, analysis, or evaluation tied to a U.S. Department of Education–funded program.
• Without parental consent, students may not be required to take surveys revealing sensitive information (political/religious beliefs, mental health, sexual behavior, self-incriminating behavior, privileged relationships, income, etc.).
• Questions and results of district-wide, state/federally mandated, and grade-level surveys must be posted publicly on the district website, with parent notification — subject to FERPA before any release.

Other updates
Update 22.02 revises Policy 2004 (Extracurricular Activities); additional numbered updates follow across policy sections.
https://media.karpowitsch.org/troysd-boa.verbose:
What this is
This document is the March 2026 policy-and-administrative-regulations update memo from MJ School Policy Services (a Miller Johnson service), addressed to policy-service clients and dated March 9, 2026. It is a standardized vendor product: proposed revisions are presented by manual section, each with an individual update number for reference, accompanied by narrative explaining the basis for each change. The memo notes its editing conventions — additions to policy or AR language are shown in red, deletions in blue, and passages highlighted in yellow require additional district-specific information to complete. It cautions that, because each district's manuals are customized, some recommendations may already be reflected locally.

Students — 2000 Series
Update 22.01 (Policy 2003, Education Records). The fiscal year 2026 State School Aid Act includes MCL 388.1764k, adding requirements for districts and ISDs regarding student surveys; adopting the revisions ensures compliance with Section 164k. Miller Johnson's supporting guidance is cited (“New Compliance Requirements Under Section 164k” and “Additional Guidance on Section 164k Compliance — District Surveys & Curriculum Requirements”). Substantively: parents may inspect all materials used for any student survey, analysis, or evaluation conducted in connection with a U.S. Department of Education–funded program; and the district will not, without parental consent, require a student to participate in a survey, analysis, or evaluation that reveals information concerning the student's or parents' political or religious affiliations/beliefs/practices; mental or psychological problems; sexual behavior or attitudes; illegal, anti-social, self-incriminating, or demeaning behavior; critical appraisals of individuals with close family relationships; legally recognized privileged relationships (lawyers, physicians, ministers); or income (except where required by law to determine program eligibility). In compliance with MCL 388.1764k, the district will make the questions and results of district-wide, state- or federally-mandated, and grade-level surveys available to the public by posting them on the district website and notifying parents — while complying with FERPA before releasing any survey results.

Other sections
The Introduction (0000) and Bylaws (1000) series have no recommended updates. Update 22.02 addresses Policy 2004 (Extracurricular Activities), with further numbered updates continuing through the manual. Districts behind on updates are urged to contact the firm (Policy@MillerJohnson.com) for compliance support.
https://media.karpowitsch.org/troysd-boa.title: March 2026 Spring Update
